"Then I shall await your wrath in good spirit," he says calmly. "Though you must understand, it is only God whose judgment for which I truly fear and hope."

"Do I?" He looks away, brow wrinkling. "Then I feel perhaps I am failing at something." 

He turns back to you, "Nonetheless, it is only God whose judgment for which I truly fear and hope."

"You are not wrong in that," Virens says. 

"And he died as he lived: through a seemingly random act of wanton violence."

He leans back again. "You've made what was once an inconvenience an immortal, captain."

"Your faith burns bright. Perhaps too bright for me, a simple man, to face unblinking." Virens adjusts his posture, uncomfortable perhaps.

"Your faith burns bright. Perhaps too bright for me, a simple man, to face unblinking." Virens adjusts his posture, uncomfortable perhaps.

"Let us speak of other things."

"I concede that he has, to my knowledge, killed many," Virens says. "And with little hesitation. By the other hand, he did speak with you; he seemed to me not a man to waste his time with talk when intent upon killing." 

"To that point," he picks up his teacup. "The deed is done. Sedge is not mine to avenge, so put any worry of that from your mind."

"You see, I am a man who does hesitate. Thoughtless action," he shakes his head. "But there are times for killing. And, after hesitation - and thought - I see that it be done."

Virens smiles unreassuringly.
options:
lke_virensKillSedgeKilledMe2:"So are you thinking about killing me?"
lke_virensWhyKillSedgeOut:Speak of other things

text:
"It is easy to dislike a great many people in a great many circumstances," Virens says. "Sedge, perhaps, easier to dislike than most, I must admit."

"Still. Death is not a thing to administer lightly."

With these words, you would be certain he has made such administrations, did not his position imply already such was the case.
options:
lke_virensKillSedgeKilledMe2:"So are you thinking about killing me?"
lke_virensWhyKillSedgeOut:Speak of other things

text:

lke_virensKillSedgeAntiAntiTechA:"I'm not sure yet."
lke_virensKillSedgeAntiAntiTechB:"In due time."
lke_virensKillSedgeAntiAntiTechC:"No. You seem different."

text:

You detect more than a hint of criticism in these words.
options:
lke_virensKillSedgeBruteA:"He was a murderer. The world is better off without him."
lke_virensKillSedgeBruteB:"How can you criticize me while Pathers raid and kill?"
lke_virensKillSedgeBruteC:"That's rich coming from a Pather. Remember Mairaath?"
lke_virensWhyKillSedgeOut:Speak of other things

text: "Ah." Virens leans back, examining you coolly. "Am I next?"
options:
lke_virensKillSedgeAntiAntiTechA:"I'm not sure yet."
lke_virensKillSedgeAntiAntiTechB:"In due time."
lke_virensKillSedgeAntiAntiTechC:"No. You seem different."

text:
"Ah," Virens leans back, examining you coolly.

"It will take a great deal of blood indeed to wash all that is impure from the Path, and indeed from the Sector entirely."
options:
lke_virensKillSedgePurity2a:"So be it."
lke_virensKillSedgePurity2b:"Not so much once a few are made example of."
lke_virensWhyKillSedgeOut:Speak of other things

text:
"You use this word 'ally'. It is, I think, too strong a word for what was between he and I."

Virens switches his teacup to his other hand, "Nor shall I deny the association, not once or thrice. The Path can be a twisted thing, captain, and ever am I surprised by the company with whom I walk."

"I apologize for nothing but for providing you with an unsatisfactory answer." He pours more tea, "My wife always said I was a middling host."

"Yes," Virens pauses, studying you. "I know."

"I have met this man, Bornanew, and I know where he is." He leaves the statement hanging.
options:
lke_virensLookingBornanewWhere:"Will you tell me where he is?"
lke_virensLookingBornanewWait:Let it hang; he knows what you want

text:
With a small, amused nod Virens acknowledges that you've left the gap in conversation for him, not you, to fill.

"I feel that I have some responsibility to Bornanew, you see." Virens flicks a nonexistent speck from his teacup. "Not for him in particular, but for courtesy, shall we say. My meeting with him was brief, and we have had no business or acquaintance since."

"I may," says Virens.

"I feel that I have some responsibility to Bornanew." He flicks a nonexistent speck from his teacup. "Not for any particular loyalty. For the general principle. My meeting with Bornanew was brief, and we have had no business or acquaintance since."

"He did, however, tell me why he was sent here. And by who." Virens looks for your reaction.

"I would not wish to direct you to him if your intentions were... unkind."
options:
lke_virensIntentionsA:"Archcurate Jaspis wants him back. To serve her. To serve the Church."
lke_virensIntentionsB:"Whatever his mission was, it's over. The archcurate is... worried about him."
lke_virensIntentionsC:"Well, I don't get paid if I don't return with him. Or his body."

text:
You convene your senior officers to discuss an extraction scenario for Jethro Bornanew, presumably employed or taken hostage by Nile Virens. 

It is impossible to completely avoid the MUA's defense network, although even cursory scans show that it is deployed to counter internal uprisings rather than external raids. Persean League casualties can be minimized, but certain sensor stations and defensive batteries will have to be disabled to open the way for a clean assault on Virens' bases.

The Pathers are another problem entirely: separate infiltration squads must simultaneously breach secure compounds located in densely populated settlements. Casual use of heavy weaponry among civilians would attract unwanted interference both in the immediate operation and your future activities. You must strike everywhere swiftly, unerringly, and with a light touch.

You oversee the raid from your bridge, your $shipOrFleet providing EW cover and sporadic fire support for the breaching drones and marine shuttles.

The raid hinges on mere minutes of chaos, comms throughout the hemisphere crackling with static and only tightbeam relays able to pierce the rain of EM scatter amid clouds of chaff and disruption. Casualty reports roll in, displays on your bridge bleeding to fill the space with a bloody glow.

In the confusion, more than one team reports success. This can be sorted out later. As quickly as they landed, your forces perform an orderly withdrawal, laying covering fire while hauling casualties and prisoners.

text:
Multiple prisoners have been taken and returned to your $shipOrFleet. None matches the admittedly crude biometric data you have on Jethro Bornanew.

Nile Virens himself, however, awaits you in a cell.

More than anything, Nile Virens looks tired. Not surprised; he seems to have been expecting you. But tired.

His fine suit, hand sewn from the looks of it, is ripped and dirty. A medic has applied some derm patches; you've already been briefed on his status. Minor lacerations, contusions; nothing serious.

options: lke_virensRaidWhoTold:"Who told you I'm looking for Jethro Bornanew."
lke_virensRaidGetTea:"Guard, get this man some tea."
lke_virensRaidWalked:"It looks like I just walked all over your 'path', huh."
lke_virensRaidToBusiness:"So you're not going to call me a servant of Moloch? Spawn of Mammon?"
lke_virensRaidNotPather:"You don't sound like much of a Pather."
lke_virensRaidCotton2:"I need to speak with Brother Cotton. To give him a message."
lke_virensRaidCotton3:"Send him a message. Tell him I need to meet with him."

text:
A quiet minute passes while some tea is retrieved. Virens stares at you, and you at him.

The guard sets a pot, and two cups down. Virens lifts his hands - bound in a restraint cuff - to the table.
options:
lke_virensRaidTeaA:Pour the tea.
lke_virensRaidTeaB:Let him pour.
lke_virensRaidTeaC:"Guard, remove those cuffs."

text:
Virens closes his eyes for a long moment, like this is making him even more tired.

"You. You did. You asked everyone on Mazalot where to find Jethro Bornanew."

He opens his eyes again. "When the captain of an independent $shipOrFleet comes to Mazalot and asks for someone, I hear of it. Especially after they've done the same thing on Chalcedon."

He smirks wearily.

"Yes," he says, "I can see that all too well, Captain $playerName. You appear to believe that I have him stashed away somewhere."

He smirks, though his eyes remain fixed on yours with... yes, that looks like rage long-suppressed, held in a little ball; a spark to light fires for years and years.

"Captain $playerName. I can see that you are not unaccustomed to the use of force. I am not so young a man as I was and have found I have little patience for theater when it comes to matters of... business."

"You want Jethro Bornanew. You believe that I have him working for me, or I've kidnapped him for some purpose."

You pour tea in the two cups.

"Blessed peace upon us," Virens says, his voice thick with irony. "I accept your hospitality, and the welcome of your home."

He lifts his cuffed hands to awkwardly pour tea in the two cups, one after the other.

"Blessed peace upon us," Virens says, his voice thick with irony. "I offer my hospitality, and the welcome of my home: Mazalot, world of dust."

He lifts his injured hands to pour tea in the two cups, one after the other. He spills only a little.

"Blessed peace upon us," Virens says, his voice thick with irony. "I offer my hospitality, and the welcome of my home: Mazalot, world of dust."

The guard moves to Virens, holds the cuffs, and gives a nod to the guard watching through the surveillance feed. A remote command unlocks the cuffs. The guard pulls the cuffs away. Virens wrists are deeply bruised.

He rubs them, eyes still fixed on you.
He uses the cuffs to shove one teacup toward you, and takes the other in his hands and lifts it, a splash escaping onto the table.
Virens picks up his teacup. His grip is unsteady, and he splashes some tea onto his hand and the table.
He blows on the tea and takes a sip.

The tea is very hot, and not very good. Some concoction of genmod yeasts grown in a vat spinning around some Persean world or other.

Virens matches your action and shows no sign of noticing the quality of the tea.
"No."

"I see no need to perform for you, captain."

He sighs, "You want Jethro Bornanew, I want to return to my home. Let us speak of business."

This he didn't expect. He looks at you, searching, now.

"I know of him," he says. "I would not say I know him. If you are looking for him, I will not be of much use. We... follow different Paths."

Virens shrugs awkwardly, the cuffs restraining his movement.

"As I said, Livewell Cotton is no associate of mine, nor am I a packet-carrier. I have ways of sending him messages, and I can do so if that is what you demand."

Virens shrugs.

"As I said, Livewell Cotton is no associate of mine. Nor am I a packet-carrier. I do have ways of sending him messages, and I can do so if that is what you demand."
"It shall bring me great pleasure to do so," Virens says, the bilious undercurrent impossible to miss, "upon my return to what is left of my home."

"I believe I can answer your questions," Virens says, "If you would allow me to explain the situation."

He clears his throat, ending in an uncomfortable cough, then begins. "Bornanew came to this world asking about the Luddic Path. He had asked similar questions on Chalcedon."
options:
lke_virensRaidJethro2a:"He met with Sedge."
lke_virensRaidJethro3:Let him tell it

text: He raises an eyebrow. "Yes. He met with Sedge. I did not know this when he came here, but it was soon confirmed by my people."

He raises an eyebrow. "Yes. He met with Sedge. The man you shot in the back, in a teahouse catering to spacers."

Virens doesn't seem to infuse his last words with any particular judgment.

He raises an eyebrow. "Yes. He met with Sedge. The man you... saw dead in the alley behind a teahouse catering to spacers."

Virens doesn't seem to infuse his last words with any particular judgment.
"I invited Bornanew for tea." He pauses, "You could learn much from the hospitality of the Luddic Faithful."
"I invited Bornanew for tea." He pauses, "My hospitality was not quite like yours. We do things differently among the Faithful."
"I invited Bornanew for tea." He pauses, "Much like you have invited me, though I must confess that my 'invitation' was gentler by far."

"Anyway; we spoke. He told me why he came to Mazalot - the real reason - and who sent him." He gives you a moment to take that in. "I did not offer him employment in any respect. Nor, as it happens, did he want it."
"We come now to the crux of our dealing, captain. I know where Bornanew went next. I am willing to tell you, provided you give me my freedom."
options:
lke_virensRaidJethroFree:"Agreed. Bornanew for your freedom." (truth)
lke_virensRaidJethroLie:"Agreed. Bornanew for your freedom." (lie)

text: In response, Virens drops his cuffed hands on the table with a clunk. He looks to you expectantly.
notes: # not wasting time quibbling about this

text:

He rubs his bruised wrists. "I don't know the coordinates by heart. I shall need a datapad and a standard map of Mazalot."

text: You leave the brig and return to your command center.

Your sensors officer and analysis team perform a thorough scan of the coordinates given to you by Nile Virens.

The location is a lightly populated, poorly developed rural zone on the far fringe of one of the more unremarkable Luddic-majority settlements. The land is nearly barren and bereft of valuable mineral deposits.

Orbital imaging shows a small, struggling farmstead close to the coordinates given. An individual human appears to be present, though it would require observation over a greater time period to fully establish an accurate count of inhabitants. There are also what appear to be a collection of burial sites near the farmstead.

"At least," your sensors officer reports, "that's what it appears to be. Weapons, people; many things can be hidden. Especially with the interference caused by this magnetic field..."
You order a shuttle prepared for descent to the coordinates provided to you by Nile Virens.
options:
lke_bornanewShuttle1:Take the shuttle to the surface
lke_bornanewVisitScan:Order a scan of Bornanew's coordinates
lke_prepMarines:Bring a squad of marines as backup
lke_bornanewAbort:Scrub the launch and consider your options

script:

SetTooltipHighlights lke_prepMarines 6
SetEnabled lke_prepMarines false

text:
You order a half-dozen of your most experienced marines to don surface warfare stealth-op gear.

Their outer suits shimmer and rustle, and six sleek black marines in light armor transform before you into a dusty haze, like a fiery, killing wind of divine vengeance out of some ancient Luddic myth.

You have to but whisper the code-phrase and they will perform a rapid assault to secure your position from most forms of military equipment available on Mazalot.

The spectra of charged particles interacting with Mazalot's atmosphere flare and break like ocean waves.

Your shuttle's descent is smooth, your pilot interfacing with the lackadaisical local traffic control and receiving only token objection to landing near a Luddic-majority settlement.

"Near" is a relative term, of course; you're well over the horizon from all but a half-dozen of the prefab hab units that have been dragged out here and modified by their inhabitants. Half of those appear to be abandoned, the surrounding fields rippled with dust.
Your shuttle lands, kicking up a cloud that wafts outward in a diminishing wave over a desolation of erratic boulders and ashy-looking trees.
Your shuttle lands, and in a blur your marine squad fans out into the surrounding plain, a desolation of erratic boulders and ashy-looking trees.

Before you is a modified prefab hab. A corroded, leaking irrigation system shudders unsteadily as it tends to an unhealthy-looking garden grid. The adjacent crop field is quite dead.

On the flight in, the shuttle sensors tagged a living human figure in the sheltered grove nearby.

You follow a path toward the grove. Protected from the parching winds by a low rise, it seems that the trees planted here collect almost enough moisture to survive.

A new run of ceramolith pipe follows the trail to the grove. Like someone wanted to water the trees.

You follow a path toward the grove. Sheltered from the dry winds by a low rise, it seems that the trees planted here collect almost enough moisture to survive. But not quite.

Your marines whisper tactical call-and-response in your ear. "$PlayerSirOrMadam," the squad leader addresses you personally, "Target ID confirmed; visual match with Bornanew. Appears unarmed."

script:

AddStoryPoints 1
BeginConversation bornanew
$player.metBornanew = true
FireAll LKEjethroOptions
text:
The shade of the grove is speckled by light filtering through thin leaves.

You find a large man in a long, dusty hooded coat, almost a robe. His back is to you.

There are a handful of Luddic grave-markers here; brightly painted with natural pigments. These look well maintained. 

The man's head is bowed, his hands clasped in prayer.

options: lke_jethroC:"I'm taking you back to Gilead."
lke_jethroWhat:"What happened with infiltrating the Path? You had a mission."
lke_jethroWhy:"Why does Jaspis want you back so badly?
lke_jethroDead:"If you like, I can have found you dead here."
lke_jethroAngels:"Wait- what was that about the 'song of angels'?"

text:
"Oh, um." He looks at you to see if you're really asking this, now. You are.

"There are... writings around Ludd. From the early disciplines, recorded later. They tend to be heavily, ah, corrected toward the Church's rulings in accordance with Ludd's wishes. Or what is known of them. Others, unofficial copies, have been created by con-artists and heretics to prove their own schemes and cults. These, the Knights purge. Some are kept as examples. For teaching; heretic-finding."

He shakes his head, "Anyway. The song of angels."
"To put it crudely, in some accounts it is said that God spoke to the Prophet Ludd through... through singing only Ludd could hear."

options: lke_jethroAngelsTalkA2:"It sounds like a neurological issue."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkB:"There was a time I heard... distant music. Like a choir."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkB:"I have heard... distant music. Like a choir. Wasn't in telemetry."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkC:"It's been said that Brother Livewell Cotton hears music. 'A choir of angels', those were the words."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkD:"Brother Livewell Cotton told me he has heard music. A 'choir of angels', he said."

text: "To be so blessed must be a burden beyond all knowing," Bornanew says grimly.

"It could," Bornanew says, and looks to you. "Where between madness and the divine stands a prophet?"

"-On uneasy ground," he finishes; quoting something with a hint of a smile.
"It is not the time for theology," Bornanew says agreeably.
He raises an eyebrow at you.

options: lke_jethroAngelsTalkTellZig:"I heard the music when we encountered a strange ship. It used exotic technologies unknown to even the Domain."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkTellGates:"I heard the music when we scanned the Gates, the Domain Gates, using a new method."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkTellZigOrbit:"That ship, the Ziggurat, is in orbit. In my fleet."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkOmen:"What am I supposed to do with a 'fearful omen'?"
lke_jethroAngelsPOLa:"Walk the Path of Light? That doesn't help."
lke_jethroAngelsPOLb:"I will try to walk the path of light. When the time comes."
lke_jethroAngelsPOLc:"'Path of light'? Brother Cotton told me the same thing."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkOut:"Let us speak of other things."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkOut:"Never mind. It's not important now."

text: "I apologize," he says. "After you came all this way, I have only the words of a novice to offer you. No great secrets or wisdom."
"I pray for your strength," he says.
"Truly?" Bornanew shakes his head, "Now it is I who is tested by a fearful omen."
"That sounds like a fearful omen," Bornanew says earnestly.
Almost unconsciously he looks up to the sky, and says "Ludd's grace preserve us."

He lets out a sigh.

"Reason as well as life are gifts of the Creator. We are tested by confrontation with divine mystery. We must decide if we follow the highest path, or merely the most convenient."

He speaks more quietly, "It is a test. When the time comes, will you walk the path of light?"

"The doctrine of the Church tells us the Gates are a product of technological sin. I'm sure you know this," Bornanew says, watching you carefully. "Ludd's words, as far as we know them, do not put it quite so simply."

options:
lke_jethroAngelsTalkTellGates2A2:"I have traversed the Gates. A lesser crossing, but I have the technology."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkTellGates2a:"The Gates are a gift, Bornanew. We should use them."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkTellGates2b:"The Gates are dangerous. They must be studied carefully."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkTellGates2d:"Just as well, because I intend to avoid them."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkTellGates2c:"Just as well, because I intend to avoid them." (lie)

text: "Then I beg Providence save us," Bornanew almost whispers. "I know not what door you have opened."

options:
lke_jethroAngelsTalkTellGates2a:"The Gates are a gift, Bornanew. We should use them."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkTellGates2b:"The Gates are dangerous. They must be studied carefully."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkTellGates2d:"Just as well, because I intend to avoid them."
lke_jethroAngelsTalkTellGates2c:"Just as well, because I intend to avoid them." (lie)

text: "I admit, captain, that I cannot but look upon this gift with suspicion when the bones of the Domain lie moldering around us."
"One must be careful around great and terrible works such as the Gates. Even your science suggests that observation may change both subject and observer."
"Just as well, then. But I suspect few of the great factions share your intent."
"Just as well, then," Bornanew says, as if you've both just agreed to not discuss something. "I suspect the great factions will do little to avoid them."
"Of course. It is not now the time for theology," Bornanew says, watching you carefully.
"He would not be the first to make that claim," Bornanew says. "Nor the last, I am sure. God save us if Cotton were a prophet."
He unclasps his hands and turns to you. "Yes. I am Jethro Bornanew."
He unclasps his hands and turns to you. "No," he says. He glances back at the graves. "This farm - what's left of it - they built it. So I was told."

"No," he says, looking at the graves. "I never met them. Not when they were alive, I mean."

"This farm - what's left of it - they built it. So I was told."
He unclasps his hands and turns to you, a hint of concern on his face. "I see. The Reverend Archcurate. Of course."
He unclasps his hands and turns to you. "Of course."
He unclasps his hands and turns to you, a hint of concern on his face. "I see. Jaspis, of course."
He looks over your uniform, then past you toward the ridgeline. "You came a long way. Captain, I assume?"

He looks over your uniform, then past you toward the ridgeline.

"You came a long way. And you brought warriors." He turns back to the graves, "I've counted two, so far."

"It is." He looks down at the graves. "It was."

"I've discovered that I'm a poor farmer, captain. It seems like everything withers at my touch."
He gives you a strange look. "If you really want to, I suppose."
options:
lke_jethroJeffA:"Jeff it is. More efficient."
lke_jethroJeffB:"Jeff it is. It's less... you know."
lke_jethroJeffC:"I'm not being serious. Your name is Jethro."

text: "Less Luddic, you mean," he says with a smirk. "I understand."
"Less Luddic," he says with a smirk. "I understand."

"Ah," he smiles awkwardly. 

"I apologize. This is all... not a shock, exactly. A rather large turn on the path that I have found to be my life, I suppose. It is a lot to reflect upon, now that it is happening."

"I failed," he says.

After a moment, he speaks again, "And I confess, I wanted to fail. After meeting the recruiter on Chalcedon..." Bornanew shakes his head.
options:
lke_jethroWhatSedge:"Sedge?"
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled:"Sedge? Don't worry, I killed him."

text:
"I failed," he says.

After a moment, he speaks again, "And I confess, I wanted to fail. After meeting the recruiter on Chalcedon..."

Bornanew shakes his head.

"Yes," he looks surprised, "That was his name. I did not like the man. I did not like the things I would have to do to make him like me."

Bornanew looks away, "He seemed to be someone I might have been if I had lived a different life. Walked a different path."
options:
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled:"Don't worry, I killed Sedge."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2A:"The deed is done. Or not done, in your case. Let it go."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2B:"But you're not him. That's why Jaspis wants you back."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2C:"You'll have to learn to be a lot less squeamish if you're to be a Knight."

text:
"You- what?" He looks you in the eyes, to confirm that you are telling the truth. He quickly discovers that you are.

"I have no words," he says, shaking his head.
options:
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilledA:"Aren't you a Knight Initiate? How can you be so squeamish?"
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilledB:"Don't worry about it."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilledC:"You could try 'thank you'."

text:
"I might not be when I get back," he admits.

"But you're right. It is probable that a Knight will be called upon to kill to defend the Faith. It is what I agreed to, what I swore I would learn."
options:
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2A:"Look, this one was on me. Don't worry about it."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2B:"See? This is why you're the man for whatever Jaspis is up to."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2C:"It gets easier with practice."

text:
"I feel that I must," he says. "In one sense, his death is due to my choices, though I had no way of knowing it."

"It is true also that he was, by all accounts - and indeed in my judgment - an evil man. His faith twisted in upon itself. It shielded him from remorse for his... actions."
options:
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2A:"You're worrying about it. I told you not to."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2B:"See? This is why you're the man for whatever Jaspis is up to."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2C:"It gets easier with practice."

text:
"I cannot do that," he says.

"I do concede that he was, by all accounts and indeed my judgment, an evil man. His faith, it was twisted in upon itself. It shielded him from remorse for his... actions."
options:
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2A:"... You're welcome."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2B:"See? This is why you're the man for whatever Jaspis is up to."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2C:"It gets easier with practice."

text: "I cannot do that," he says.
options:
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2A:"... You're welcome."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2B:"See? This is why you're the man for whatever Jaspis is up to."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled2C:"It gets easier with practice."

text: He nods hesitantly, and says "Then I will speak no further on this matter."
He gives you a skeptical look, "That worries me all the more."
"Your words are a cold comfort, captain."

"The Reverend Archcurate," he says automatically.

"Sorry," he adds quickly. "Habit. They really drill it into you in the Church. Archcurate Jaspis... told me I could call her Sophronia, as if I was family."

"She wants to change the path of the Faith, I believe. She believes I can help her do it."

options: lke_jethroWhyOptB:"People died for me to find you."
lke_jethroWhatSedgeKilled:"I shot a man to find you."
lke_jethroWhyOptD:"No one else seems to think much of you."

text:
"So was Ludd," he says, and laughs. 

"Ah," he catches himself, and wipes his eyes. "Forgive me my blasphemy. I do not believe I am Ludd; I do not hear God's word in the songs of angels."
He sighs deeply. "Was it necessary?"
options:
lke_jethroWhyOptBnotImp:"That's not important now. I have a mission to complete."
lke_jethroWhyOptByes:"Yes. In conflicts between the powerful, that's how it works."
lke_jethroWhyOptBno:"No. I may have overreacted."

text: "Gilead," he says, staring at the graves.
He looks at you uneasily, but says nothing.
"I see," he looks at you uneasily.

He shrugs. "The Reverend Archcurate does. And that was enough to bring you here, for me."

He glances over at you, "I don't care to speculate about how many credits she's wasting on me."

"Yes," he says and glances at you. Expecting some news of Jaspis' health, maybe. He turns away again.

"Time comes for us all. I expect she sees that. She wants to leave behind a world... that's better, I think."
options:
lke_jethroWhyOptEa:"An admirable cause."
lke_jethroWhyOptEb:"A hopeless dream."
lke_jethroWhyOptEc:Ne rien dire.

text: He grunts acknowledgement, looking down at the brightly painted graves.
He doesn't respond. Just looks down at the brightly painted graves.

He quickly turns to stare at you with a strange intensity. "I-," he begins, and his words catch in his throat. He breathes heavily for a moment, almost unsteady on his feet.

"No," he says. "I'm coming with you to Gilead. It's what I have to do."
options:
lke_jethroDeadHopelessA:"Feeling okay?"
lke_jethroDeadHopelessB:Give him a moment
lke_jethroDeadHopelessC:"Don't die for real on me, now."

text: "No," he says, then "Yes, I mean. I'm fine. Just, the answer is no. I'm coming with you to Gilead. It's what I have to do."
"No," he says. "The answer is no. I'm coming with you to Gilead. It's what I have to do."
He quickly turns to stare at you with a strange intensity. "I-," he begins, and his words catch in his throat. He breathes heavily for a moment, almost unsteady on his feet.
options:
lke_jethroDead1a:"Feeling okay?"
lke_jethroDead1b:Give him a moment
lke_jethroDead1c:"Don't die for real on me, now."

text:
After a moment, he lets a breath out, and then almost - but not quite - casually wipes a sleeve across his face. His eyes are red, you see.

"Yes. Yes, I am well. Is your offer serious? You would do this thing for me?"

He lets a breath out, and then almost casually wipes a sleeve across his face. His eyes are red, you see.

"Forgive me," he clears his throat. "Is your offer serious? You would do this thing for me?"

After a moment, he lets a breath out, and then almost - but not quite - casually wipes a sleeve across his face. His eyes are red, you see.

"Yes. Yes, I am well. Is your offer serious? You would do this thing for me?"

options:
lke_jethroDeadOut1:"Yes. Just say the word."
lke_jethroDead3a:"Of course. I just need a body to substitute." (look pointedly at the graves)
lke_jethroDead3b:"I'm afraid I cannot. The Archcurate gave me a mission and I intend to complete it."

text:
"No," he says with a sudden ferocity.

He catches himself, then begins again, more calmly, 
"And no, forget about all of that. I'm coming with you to Gilead. I have to."
"Of course," he says quietly. "I have a duty; I made a promise."

He looks, struck silent, at the light coming through the trees.

"Let someone else walk my path."

He turns to you. "I accept. Tell Jaspis... tell her... I should write something, maybe..."
options:
lke_jethroDeadOut2b:"No. You must live for yourself now."
lke_jethroDeadOut2a:"You should. It will help. She was... emotional."

text:
He sighs, covering his face with his hands. "I can't do this. I'm sorry."

"I have a duty. I made a promise. It's what I have to do."

"But..." he falls silent.

In a long minute, he appears to accept your words.
"This is it then," he turns and looks at you once more.
options:
lke_jethroDeadOut3a:"It is. Burn bright, burn long." (leave)
lke_jethroDeadOut3b:"It is. Blessed be your path." (leave)
lke_jethroDeadOut3c:"It is. Keep your head down." (leave)

One of those unhappy little events that Providence provides to bring an unexpected end to the path of life... and which burns away obvious identifying features.

The Reverend Archcurate will accept a closed-casket burial, you think.

id: # LKE return to Gilead with Bornanew

options: lke_returnToGileadJaspis2:"I found Bornanew. He's here, with my $shipOrFleet."

text:
"Heavens rejoice!" A ray of unselfconscious joy breaks through the Archcurate's well-tended walls.

"You must bring him here, I must see him," she pauses, then laughs at herself. "You know what I mean. Come now, I shall have the Knights forgo their accustomed rigidity."

She cuts her end of the comm-link with unusual speed, leaving you with a static holding image provided by the Church: a human silhouette descending to Gilead on a beam of light.

An implausible image at best.

id: # Now, actually take him to Jaspis

options: 3:lke_shuttleDownBornanew:Take a shuttle with Bornanew to the surface

text:
Your shuttle is given an unusually expedient flight path to the complex housing Jaspis' office.

Bornanew watches the green world of Gilead grow below you.
options:
lke_endingJaspisStart:Let him watch
lke_endingStartA:"It's no Mazalot, is it."
lke_endingStartB:"How are you doing, $global.jethroName?"

text:
He smiles, acknowledging the absurdity of your statement.

"Mazalot wasn't all bad. There was a sort of freedom there that can't be found on Gilead, though much danger as well."

He turns back to the view, "I did miss the green."

He smiles politely.

"Apprehensive, I confess. I believe I shall be punished by the Knights, though the Reverend Archurate may try to shield me. I shall ask her not to. An oath is an oath."

"I suspect there will be much silent contemplation and scrubbing of floors." He looks thoughtful, "They do like that sort of thing. I don't mind it."

He turns back to the face of Gilead.

You are met on the landing pad by a solemn Luddic Knight and attending Knight Initiate. The Knight's frown deepens noticeably as they take in Bornanew. 

Knight Ailanthus leads you to Archcurate Jaspis' office in otherwise judgmental silence. Bornanew takes this treatment in stride, enjoying the gardens and fresh air.

script:
BeginConversation jaspis
ShowSecondPerson bornanew
AdjustRepPerson jaspis FRIENDLY 15
text:
As you enter Jaspis' office, Knight Ailanthus inhales to announce you - and the Archcurate interrupts. 

"Jethro, here you are!" she says, "Providence provides!" Knight Ailanthus lets out a cough, and Jaspis responds by waving them away.

"As well, I must attest, by way of the efforts of the good captain whose footsteps I hear also," she says, smiling, "For lest we forget, the Path of the righteous will not walk upon itself, is that not so?"

Bornanew takes Jaspis' small, frail hand in his own.

"Yes, of course. Please, Mother Sophronia, you must not get too excited. It is just me, and I have caused you much distress."

"Yes and truly, " she says, shaking Bornanew off. "And for it you've already decided to undergo some senseless display of penance, I doubt not. An unseemly habit learnt from the Knights."

"Captain," she turns toward you. "You must grow tired of this, so I shall be succinct: you have my deepest gratitude. My secretary shall see that you receive your payment."

She aims herself at the doorway, "Degory, attend!"

As you leave with Degory, you hear Jaspis address Bornanew.

"The balance of power is changed; both I and the Faith's future have need of you. But..."

"Why 'Jeff'?" she asks.

You don't hear his reply.

script:

$option = lke_endingWrapup2 0
FireBest DialogOptionSelected
text:
Bornanew puts his hands together and nods his head.

Blind to his response, Jaspis begins to address him, "The balance of power is changed; both I and the Faith's future have need of you..."

text:
The soft-spoken secretary, Degory, produces an incongruous datapad from her robes and conducts the transfer of the Archcurate's payment to your accounts. She then summons the grim-faced Knight Ailanthus to escort you to your waiting shuttle. 

Your return to orbit is uneventful.

id: # LKE generic Jaspis talk during mission

text:
You speak first with some kind of secretary who politely persuades you to wait patiently.

After a short time, the image of the Archcurate Jaspis appears. She makes little effort to look toward comms feed as she putters around her office.

"It is good to hear from you, captain," she says. "Have you found Novice Bornanew?"
"Then I shall pray that Providence sees fit to give you a path both short and safe."

options: BFIN_start2b:"A call from Jeff? Of course, put him through."

text:
"Captain $playerName. I do not forget your assistance." He makes a crooked smile.

The link is poor-quality; his voice is hollowed, lacking the warmth you remember.

"I would ask, beg if I must, for your service once more."

"What I ask, it is a part of the penance I must serve." He raises a hand, as if to stop your objections, "I do also believe it will also serve a spiritual and intrinsic good." 

"And," he clears his throat, "for your assistance, a substantial reward of credits has been authorized by the Reverend Archcurate Jaspis."

"The service..." He releases a breath. "An object considered holy by some in the mother Church has appeared in the possession of a wealthy collector."
options:
BFIN_start3a:"There's been a theft?"
BFIN_start3b:"Did this collector not make a large enough tithe?"

text:
"No," he says, looking up. "That would have been simple. This object, this 'relic', is by all appearances still untouched on Tartessus, in the archives of the Cathedral of Holy Exodus."

"The curates of the archive were in some panic at word that they had lost a holy relic. They checked very, very carefully. I too have personally confirmed that it remains in their keeping."
options:
BFIN_start4a:"So it's a fake. No problem."
BFIN_start4b:"And this is embarrassing to the Church?"

text: "Ah," he gives a weary smile. "The collector is quite secular, and has no relationship with the Church."

"But it could be," $global.jethroName says hesitantly. "It raises... questions."

"And those may become a problem. It is a complex situation and potentially dangerous."

"It could be," $global.jethroName says. "This collector has not been silent about his new acquisition."

"Naturally, the Church does not approve. At the same time, mere disapproval is no reason to cause a diplomatic incident, to say nothing of... well, it is a complex situation. And potentially dangerous."

"If you choose to assist me," he says, "then I shall share with you what, ah, 'intel' I have managed to piece together."

He looks almost embarrassed for a moment, then forces himself proceed. "Credits, yes. I have also been instructed to offer a payment of 50,000 credits when - if - you join my investigation, and an additional 50,000 credits upon completion of my quest."

"If it is possible." He sighs, "And that I cannot promise."

options: BFIN_askCredits:"50k down, and how much on completion? You didn't say."
notes: # don't waste time

options: BFIN_paidYou:"50,000 credits? That's what Jaspis paid me to get you back."
BFIN_remindReward:"Remind me, there was mention of payment..."
BFIN_askConflicts:"Before I agree, I need to know of potential factional conflicts."
BFIN_askConflicts:"Now that I've agreed, I need to know of any potential factional conflicts."
BFIN_dontConsider:"It sounds like you don't consider this object to be a legitimate holy relic."
BFIN_howPenance:"Just how much penance are they going to make you do?"
BFFI_accept:"I accept. I shall assist you in your, uh, quest."
BFFI_shuttleBornanew:Arrange a shuttle for Bornanew and close the comms
cutCommLinkPolite:Close the comms, but don't send a shuttle just yet

BFIN_notNow:"I will take more time to consider your request, $global.jethroName."

text: "Truly?" He sighs, deflated at this revelation. "The arighting of one errant novice cannot be worth that sum."

"My apologies," he says, opening a battered notebook. "I am unused to this sort of, of bookkeeping."

"Yes, 50,000 upfront, and another 50,000 credits upon resolution. It is hoped that the false artifact might be removed from consideration, one way or another."

He gives you a questioning look. "Without causing an interfactional incident, that is."

"Of course," he says, opening a battered notebook. "The Church anticipates expenses. Well," he grimaces, "Reverend Archcurate Jaspis does."

"Yes, 50,000 upfront, and another 50,000 credits upon satisfactory resolution. It is hoped that the false artifact might be removed from consideration, one way or another."

He gives you a questioning look. "Without causing an interfactional incident, that is."

"This is only the beginning, I am certain," he says with resigned humor. "Violation of Knightly vows is no small falsity, and I am uncertain even that I am truly meant to 'solve' this task set before me."

He pauses, then speaks more quietly, "I fear that my failure might be intended to chasten Jaspis. The Reverend Archcurate, I mean." He looks upward, and lets out a sigh. "She has put altogether too much faith in me."

"It is... trying," he says. "I am not, I am sure, meant to 'solve' this quest set before me to my violation of Knightly vows. Remorse, contrition... those I give freely, and wholly."

He speaks more quietly, "I speculate that my failure and disgrace is being arranged to humble the influence of the Reverend Archcurate Jaspis." He shakes his head, "She has put altogether too much faith into me."

"A reasonable concern." Bornanew rubs his chin. "First and without doubt, a notable member of a Persean League gens is involved." 

"The rest are deduction and speculation." He starts counting off fingers, "The Cathedral of Holy Exodus has much to lose if corruption is revealed in their house; I suspect an insider or an infiltrator."

Next finger, "Within the greater Church, there is a loosely organized sect who, ah, back the theological legitimacy of relics. They would doubtless have forceful opinions on the subject of a duplicate relic, and that in secular hands."

Another finger, "The Luddic Path has much to gain from discord within the Church, and they have proximity to... the problem."

Next finger, "Though it is possible that the criminal underworld has found a means of profit here, possibly Kanta's organization. The pirate queen has an especial taste for desecration, though has demonstrated no particular hatred for the Church beyond her habitual misanthropy." He lets his hands fall.

"Beyond that, I grasp for tenuous threads, captain."

"Um. It is so obvious?" Bornanew almost looks guilty. Almost.

"The Reverend Archcurate did say that my feelings on the subject are... not easily veiled."

"This investigation requires discretion." He brings his hands together. "I shall be mindful of my disposition."

Bornanew puts his hands together, and lowers his head for a moment.

"I thank you for that, captain. In the meantime, I will do what research and inquiries I can with the resources I am provided."

"If you wish to assist my quest, then come to Gilead. The Knights have provided me an entry in the comms directory in order to further my penance."

Bornanew puts his hands together, and lowers his head.

"I understand. If you change your mind, come to Gilead. The Knights have provided me an entry in the comms directory in order to further my penance."

Bornanew puts his hands together, and lowers his head.

"I thank you for that, captain. In the meantime, I will do what research and inquiries I can with the resources I am provided."

"If you wish to assist me in my quest, contact me here on Gilead. The Knights have provided me an entry in the comms directory in order to further my penance."

Bornanew puts his hands together, and lowers his head for a moment.

"I understand. If you change your mind, return here, to Gilead. The Knights have provided me an entry in the comms directory in order to further my penance."

text:
Bornanew looks almost surprised at your words. He does not pray, or thank Providence. He simply looks directly at you through the waver of the comm-link.

"Thank you, captain. Against all odds, you've cast a light upon my path toward redemption."

Perhaps apologetic for the moment of earnestness, he quickly adds, "I am prepared to leave when you are ready for me. I shall await word of your shuttle."
Traffic control is mercifully efficient when arranging transport for a Knight of Ludd.

id: # BFFI - Bornanew Files, case of the False Idols

text:
Before long, Novice Bornanew steps aboard your flagship carrying only a small satchel of his possessions.

Your TriPad pings acknowledgement of a funds transfer from the Church of Galactic Redemption.

text: "You have my thanks, captain," he says. "That will serve my needs most adequately."

text: Bornanew nods, not quite a bow. "You are right, of course."
Bornanew nods, not quite a bow. "My apologies, I did not intend to spurn your hospitality. It is a virtue."
"If you would, please," Bornanew says with a nod that isn't quite a bow. "It would be most appropriate for my service of penance."
You have Bornanew's luggage taken to his quarters by a couple crew who clearly expected heavier lifting, then have him meet you in a debriefing room to plan your next move.

script:
ShowPersonVisual
Highlight "Menes Yaribay"
$option = BFFI_onBoardingYarbResp 0
FireBest DialogOptionSelected
text:
Novice Bornanew speaks from the head of the table on which he has placed a paper notebook. He seems disinclined to use the shared workspace of the holo visualization suite; you're not even sure he brought a datapad.

"...collector who now possesses this duplicate relic is a man by the name Menes Yaribay, a minor member of Gens Yaribay of Kazeron," he looks to you for a sign of recognition.

Of course you know the Yaribays. You were given Horus Yaribay's diplomatic credentials to arrange a meeting with High Hegemon Daud.

Horus Yaribay might owe you a favor, depending on how he views the situation.

Of course you know the Yaribays. You were given Horus Yaribay's diplomatic credentials to arrange a meeting with High Hegemon Daud and have, on at least one occasion, used those credentials to call a favor on account of Gens Yaribay's prestige.

Hopefully he didn't find out.
options:
BFFI_onBoardingYarb2confess:Explain everything to $global.jethroName
BFFI_onBoardingYarb2noConfess:Explain, but leave out the abuse of credentials part

text:
Of course you know the Yaribays. You were given Horus Yaribay's diplomatic credentials to arrange a meeting with High Hegemon Daud. During that meeting, you handed over the key required for an encrypted channel between Horus Yaribay and Baikal Daud.

Horus Yaribay might owe you a favor, depending on how he views the situation.

You know of Gens Yaribay of Kazeron, one of the three dominant ruling family-corporations. Theirs is most inclined toward rapprochement with the Hegemony, focusing less on heavy military industry and more on interfactional ventures.
options: BFFI_onBoardingYarb2:"Yes, I've heard of the Yaribays."

text:
Bornanew looks concerned as you describe your dealings. "Your conscience, your soul, is between you and- well, I am not your judge or confessor."

"From a practical standpoint, I would advise caution in asking too much of Horus of Gens Yaribay, to his face or behind it. He may know but choose not to act on your transgression."

A brief look of concern crosses Bornanew's face, and he pauses as if composing an awkward question. He puts his hand back down on the table, seeming to dismiss the notion, and responds in a different tone.
notes: # 'course he knows.

text: "This is providential," he says, nodding. "Your previous dealings with Horus Yaribay could open an easier path to resolving the Church's problem."
He nods and proceeds.

"The question, then, is how Horus Yaribay, the nominal gens leader, views Menes Yaribay's recent interest in religious artifacts."

Bornanew flips through his paper book, not really taking the time to read the dense notes therein. He runs a finger across lists of names.

"Not long ago Menes Yaribay was assigned the office of demarch, in essence the executive administrator, of Olinadu in the Kumari Kandam system. It is not a prestigious post. Olinadu is a small mining colony and the system is rife with Luddic Path activity. It is a dangerous, corrupt place."

Bornanew eye's return to you. "This is why I need your help."

options: BFFI_onBoardAskMenes:"You've done your research. Tell me about Menes Yaribay himself."
BFFI_onBoardAskInCharge:"How did a Kazeron aristocrat get put in charge of Olinadu?"
BFFI_onBoardAskRelic:"Tell me more about the relic at the center of this. What is it?"
BFFI_onBoardAskTart4:"Wait, why's the real glove on Tartessus if Ludd removed it on Gilead?"
BFFI_onBoardAskPlan:"What's the plan? Ask Menes nicely for the glove?"
BFFI_onBoardAskChalcedon:"Kumari Kandam? You were on Chalcedon. With the Path."

text:
"Indeed I was." He's steady. "You know of this. There I met Wrestling Sedge of the Luddic Path, and there I left my vows. For a time."

A pause. "You followed my path, and spoke with the man Sedge. He told you where I went from there, Mazalot, where you found me."

"Indeed I was." He's steady as he speaks. "You know of this. There I met Wrestling Sedge of the Luddic Path, and there I left my vows. For a time."

A pause. "And you killed the man Sedge," he says. "The Path will have no love for you there, but I trust that you will be prepared for... that."

"Certain tawdry media outlets in the outer League worlds and beyond report rumor and gossip about affairs, social vendettas, and... how shall I put this... decadent festivities undertaken by Menes. More so in his younger years, at least." Bornanew turns pages in his notebook, not reading, but recalling as he goes through the motions with his hands.

"His enthusiasm for such pursuits has waned from the cycles of his youth, and his interests have shifted. At heart, I suspect Menes is still a man who wishes for all to hear his name, and for all who hear it to be very impressed."

"Unfortunately for his ambition, he is a natural dilettante in all pursuits. He avoids challenge, seeking instead flattery and the appearance of success which may be purchased with credits." Bornanew reports this without obvious outward judgment, as if discussing the results of a gravimetric survey.

"To his gens, Menes has given only scandal and debt. I suspect he was placed in the Olinadu administration to limit his ability to further damage their reputation."

Bornanew smiles grimly now. "It is some irony that he has potentially stumbled into more trouble than ever."

"Gens Yaribay has a controlling interest in the military corporation which exercises the monopoly of violence for the polity of Olinadu. The Pathers are an ever-present threat, so those who don't cooperate... well, that is the stick which compels. The carrot, on the other hand, is a stream of investment credits facilitated by Gens Yaribay brokers."

"The locals cooperate with this arrangement, and agreed to vote Menes Yaribay to an executive position, perhaps understanding that he'd play the role as a figurehead rather than a serious administrator. Olinadu receives investment and safety while, from all accounts, Menes Yaribay simply wants to be liked."

"Compared to the alternatives," says Bornanew, "he is an ideal overlord."

"Yes, the... artifact." Bornanew looks down, clasping his hands together, then taking them apart and setting them deliberately on the table.

"Ludd's right-hand glove," an abashed smile appears.

"A standard work-glove of Domain manufacture. Light vac-suit compatible. The story is that Ludd removed it to touch the ground of Gilead."
options:
BFFI_onBoardAskRelic1a:"And you don't think it's real? The original, I mean."
BFFI_onBoardAskRelic1b:"Seems like a lot to get worked up about."
BFFI_onBoardAskRelic1c:"What's Ludd going to do with two right-hand gloves?"

text:
"It's a real glove," he says, "That much is true."

"It's just a work glove, one of many designs prevalent through the immediate pre- and post-Collapse Persean Sector."
"I think," he begins, "it is a matter of seeking a physical connection with the sacred."
Bornanew laughs. "Yes, exactly."

"My objection is that the glove, even if it is the glove, is not important. What is important is Ludd's message, Ludd's sacrifice; it is the act of a divine moral force touching the soul of humanity with supreme grace."

"What is a glove to the light of Revelation?" The words come out forcefully.

"Even if it were Ludd's glove - and that we can't, and should not, be sure of - would God love you more for possessing it? Touching, looking upon, and worshipping it?"

His brow furrows. "Then some would try to extract DNA from the glove. Clone Ludd, they say, to re-open a connection to God." He shakes his head furiously, "As if you could fool God with the twin of the prophet's body!"

"Or the glove that body might have worn."

"It's the fool's logic of 'magic' of the lowest kind, the heretical notion that humanity might manipulate the Divine to our own ends. Creation turned on its head!"

You've never seen Bornanew like this, his voice like a river; his physical size giving power to his words. Seeing you see this, he realizes it himself.

"I apologize for my outburst, captain," he says, looking down.
options:
BFFI_onBoardAskRelic5a:"Let's try to focus on the matter, ah, at hand."
BFFI_onBoardAskRelic5b:"No need to apologize. I can't help but agree."
bffi_onBoardAskRelic5d:"But magic isn't real. So what harm is focusing on a glove, fake relic or not?"

text: He nods, suppressing a smile, looking down at his notebook. He puts his hands together again.
He meets your gaze. "I shall interpret your words as forgiveness. It is nonetheless unseemly for a Knight to let uncontrolled passion rule their actions."

"Of course, of course." He rubs his chin, calmer now.

"The problem is not that it might work, but that such self-trickery will lead the Faithful astray from the Word. And if we don't follow the Word, then what use have we for revelation? Are we to simply discard God's gifts?"

He sits, looking into the distance somewhere. "Ludd chose the revealed path not because it was easy, and not because it was without suffering. Ludd chose because it was Good."
notes: # bffi_onBoardAskRelic5dCotton:"I've heard those words before. From Brother Cotton."

text:
He glances at you with a fleeting grimace of self-reflection.

"With further apology for the ramble through theology, captain; the matter of the false relic should be our focus."

Mention of Cotton dims Bornanew's expression. "He's read the Word, too, of course. In his way."

He sighs, deeply. "Let his crimes, worldly and spiritual, serve as bloody warning of the path those... that those uncertain of their faith might be compelled to follow. I pray it weren't so."

"Which, with apologies for the ramble through theology, brings us back to the false relic."

Bornanew gives you a look.

"It's a tedious story. And unimportant."

Bornanew closes his book of notes.

"It has been made clear to me that the Church would like this problem, this false idol, to quietly disappear," he says. "It can only tear open old rifts within the Church which would weaken the resolve of the Faithful. It would turn walker against walker in troubling times which promise naught but more trouble."

"I am prepared to ask nicely, but I do not believe Menes will agree to give someone like myself his glove."
options:
bffi_onBoardPlanToMe:"But you think he might listen to someone like me."
bffi_onBoardPlanBuy:"We could offer to buy it."
bffi_onBoardPlanSteal:"We could steal it."
bffi_onBoardPlanHorus:"I could talk to Horus Yaribay. He leads his gens, presumably."

text: "Yes," he says with the hint of a smile.
"Interesting." Bornanew rubs his chin. "If you believe Horus will be receptive to you bearing this... news. I wonder if it may be as simple as that."
"It is... yes, that is possible," he says begrudgingly. "I would like to know more before we act, however. I pray this may prove simpler than could be hoped."
"You could." Bornanew emphasizes 'you'. "The Church refuses to acknowledge the false relic."

"I propose you contact Menes directly. If you show interest in his pursuits, I believe he will be well-disposed toward someone of your... repute. He could provide us with information to plan our next move."

Bornanew shuts his notebook.
"Your repute. As you well know," he says rather definitively. "And so will Menes."

"I propose you contact Menes directly. If you show interest in his pursuits, I believe he will be well-disposed toward someone of your... repute. He could provide us with information to plan our next move."

Bornanew shuts his notebook.
options: BFFI_onBoardEndPLhostile2:"Just one problem- the League wants me shot on sight."

text:

text:
Sometimes acquiring something is as simple as taking it.

Both you and Bornanew - who is unenthusiastic, but professional throughout - are outfitted with an inconspicuous set of tools: a sheet-diamond rated cutter, EM glitcher, lockbreakers.

Bornanew sets aside some of the bulkier accoutrement with a shake of his head.

Your tech officer installs a schematic of Menes' complex and a suite of scramblers on your datapad, a remote interfacer is hidden inside a fashionable stylus.

You perform the operation of various gadgets with your armorer, and review the choreography of the heist with your tactical officer. It is not a perfect plan, but with a combination of initiative, redundancy, and exceptional access...

Bornanew is standing very still, his fist against his jaw.

"It won't work," he says.

"With all due respect, your tactician is no thief. And neither are you. It must be me."

text: "I am. I did," he says. "But more necessary than my conscience is our success. I shall bear my burden."

"Why should Menes leave you alone with the relic, or at all?" He looks over at you. 

"I am a Knight of Ludd, devoted and taciturn. I shall simply... display the power of my faith."

"Pray forgive me, captain; I do no question your faith." He presses his hands together.

"But Menes Yaribay cannot see into your soul. He will see your uniform, and my robes. That will be enough."

"We attend Menes' party. Be interesting. Be yourself. Get Menes to show off the glove." 

"I know this trade, captain. Have faith." He pauses for a moment, acknowledging some irony in his own words. "It works best if you act natural."
He crosses his arms. "I am not."

Bornanew bows his head to your words. His expression is grim.

Menes has not been discreet. As a celebration of his first cycle as the new demarch of Olinadu, he invited a holodoc team for a puff-piece interview and viewing of his 'collection'. Your tactical team reviews the holos, constructing schematic of the interior of Menes' complex, inferring layout and defenses from this and other publicly available information.

It is estimated that roughly 100 marines will be required to pull off the operation.

Your marines will be equipped with an eclectic mix of equipment to allow them to pose as high-level pirates. They will be secretly infiltrated into Olinadu as, variously, spacer crew on shore-leave, contract technicians, and ne'er-do-wells looking to spend ill-gotten gains somewhere away from heavy law enforcement.

It goes without saying that you shall need to approach Olinadu with your $shipOrFleet's transponder off. Menes' overly charitable outlook on your character and generalized inattention to his official duties should allow you to join his 'party' with little oversight about how you actually got there.

To avert suspicion and ensure optimal timing, you and Bornanew will personally attend Menes' party when the raid is executed.

The raid will commence when two of a series of randomly generated time-slots are passed without receiving a stand-down signal. This signal will be, by all appearances, a routine status query to your $shipOrFleet, and appear to be perfectly routine behaviour for a justifiably neurotic independent $shipOrFleet operator.

Bornanew is watchful throughout the planning process, only occasionally advocating for lower-force protocols when a particularly bloodthirsty marine squad leader expresses more enthusiasm than strictly required for certain tactical details.

text:
Menes' invitation provides you with a flight path and Demarch-level traffic control override codes.

It's probably a violation of Persean League ethical guidelines for you to be using these to attend a personal party, but from what you can overhear of the pilot's comms, the locals have a casual attitude toward the arrangement.

You and Bornanew make an 'undocumented' arrival on Olinadu then transfer to a private shuttle service in order to attend Menes' party.

Your hired pilot gives a shrug as she shows you a flight path blocked out with red DENIED tags - until you apply the key attached to your invitation, which turns out to be a Demarch-level override code.

It's probably a violation of Persean League ethical guidelines for you to be using these to attend a personal party, but the pilot simply smiles and proceeds. This sort of thing isn't unusual under Menes' administration, it appears.

Menes Yaribay's compound is a scrubbed-looking slab of trendy architecture set on a bluff overlooking Olinadu's primary habitation cluster.

A smattering of outbuildings and hangars, attached by fat trunks of conduit and glowing walktubes, sprawl across the less-fashionable view-angles of the compound toward the refineries and holding tanks of the planet's industrial center.

One set of hangar bay doors dutifully open, and your shuttle descends through the glowing atmo-field. Such gadgetry is an ostentatious addition to a personal residence, but it avoids the bother of visitors (and their shuttles) having to undergo pressure-cycling.

An enthusiastic Menes is already approaching your shuttle's access ramp as it sets itself down and signals the all-clear.

Bornanew exchanges a look with you, then rises to disembark.

"Captain $playerName," Menes crosses his chest with one arm, hand on heart, the other held behind, giving a small bow. "Allow me to welcome you to my home."

"And you've brought your fascinating friend!" he exclaims, as if it was a surprise. "This must be...?"

BFFI_attendParty3a:"This is $global.jethroName."
BFFI_attendParty3b:"Jethro Bornanew, a novice of the Knights of Ludd."

text: "Jethro Bornanew," he adds quickly. "Novice Bornanew of the Knights of Ludd, at your service. May this day be blessed by Providence."
"May this day be blessed by Providence," he says.

Bornanew nods, not quite a bow, with his hands held together. Perhaps to avoid Menes' much-ringed hands from grabbing his. You are not so lucky, and must endure an overlong handshake.

Menes is delighted at all of this, eyes flashing with something akin to greed.

Menes leads you past gilded statues posed to guard the portal to the main complex; the forms are evocative of splashes of molten gold frozen by happenstance into sensual humanoid figures.

You endure a rolling barrage of patter from Menes about the expense of renovations, the acquisition of artworks, the exclusivity of access to particular artists and engineering firms who have contributed to his home, his manor, and its collection.

Occasionally a robotic or human servitor appears to offer drinks or nibbles, and you've a growing feeling that Menes has picked up a glass from one only to place it back on the tray of another without ever taking a sip between the two.

A fresh glass in hand, delicate amber liquid fizzing away, Menes leads you into what can only be his prize entertaining space.

The space is at once cavernous and intimate; the sightlines and carved, wrought, and woven details calculated to replicate the appearance of cozy warmth like the den of some thoughtful patron of art, history, and literature.

A double handful of well-dressed remoras mill about, interrupting their conversations to smile and lift a glass as Menes enters. They look to be a collection of industrialists, financiers, distant gens-relatives, and at least one officer from atop the local military detachment who appears a little worse for his drink. Most nod to you and Bornanew, eyes not lingering to the point of impoliteness.

One skinny man in a finely embroidered suit has no such compunction, and rushes to greet your party, eyes agog at the newest additions to Menes' collection.

He nearly trips over himself to speak. "The great Captain $playerName! If half of what Menes has said is true... why, half the Sector must tremble at the appearance of your $shipOrFleet!"

"We'll have to ask Navarch Atreus over there what he thinks of your combat record," the man grins wickedly, "If he hasn't had too many of these, of course; with all thanks to our kind host, a true gentleman and scholar." He lifts his glass approvingly at Menes, and you see his eyes slide toward Bornanew.

"And Jethro Bornanew, I believe? A Knight of Ludd?"
"I don't recall an introduction," Bornanew says, his hands still held piously together, allowing him to deniably reject both drink and the touch of the present company.
options:
bffi_attendPartyHub1Intro4a:"Pleased to meet you...?"
bffi_attendPartyHub1Intro5b:Ne rien dire
bffi_attendPartyHub1Intro5c:"No need to be prickly, $global.jethroName, the man is being friendly."

"Of course, captain." He turns to the man. "Pray forgive me, sir, my forgotten manners," he says.

Menes laughs heartily.

"Let me introduce Ulmus Pond," he says. "Now he'll claim to be a mere commodities trader," Menes winks as if you are in on a shared joke, "but I daresay he knows everyone of any importance in the Kumari Kandam system. And if you need anything." He lowers his voice, "Anything at all. He's your man."

Pond, grinning widely, makes a brushing-off gesture as if to dismiss Menes' words - but not so assertively as to deny them.

notes: # stop winking dude

text:
Menes leads you and Bornanew to his inner sanctum, flashing a grin as he operates a series of obtrusive biometric checks.

The air cools by a degree, perhaps two, the armored mass of the walls felt as a dullness through your feet. The subdued lighting, drapery, and framing of the space suggest the interior of a bank vault or a shrine, though not to the praise of any Luddic virtue.

Quite the contrary, depending on one's outlook.

On a pedestal of carved wood, in a spot of light like holy revelation, a single glove is upthrust. It's fingers appear frozen in the midst of just starting to curl back toward a relaxed state after giving you a little wave.

"Do you know what this is?" Menes whispers to you. 

"The Right-Hand Glove of Blessed Ludd," Bornanew answers, stepping forward, voice filled with trembling reverence.

"Removed by the Blessed Ludd, whereupon that prophet fell to the sacred Earth of Gilead to kiss that holy ground. Blessings be upon Ludd; Good is the Word; By the Holy Pneuma; By the Divine Light which illuminates the righteous Path through the Garden of Temptation..."
notes: # Jeff spends a story point

text:
Bornanew himself falls to his knees, half-whispering, half-sobbing in prayer, rocking back and forth, his eyes wide and focus vague. 

This is a display you have never seen from $global.jethroName, completely at odds with his usual air of reserve.

Menes steps back with you, looking somehow appalled and delighted at the same time. He stares at Bornanew like a voyeur.
options:
bffi_mpSteal6:Attendre a bit
bffi_mpSteal5:"He's overwhelmed... you must understand, Menes."

text: "Yes, yes," he replies under his breath.

Bornanew keeps at it, absolutely rapt upon his prayer toward the glove, tears and phlegm running down his face. It is, truly, a pathetic and uncomfortably intimate sight.

Menes keeps staring, an open half-smile on his lips.
options: bffi_mpSteal7:"Menes. We should let him be for a moment, don't you think? Menes?"

text: "Hmm? Oh. Oh yes. Of course, captain," he says, still watching.
options: bffi_mpSteal8:Gently pull Menes' away and ask about other items in the collection

text:
It takes a slightly more than polite tug to overcome Menes' inertia, but finally he's dislodged from Bornanew's spectacle toward another of the handful of chambers making up this 'inner sanctum'.

Menes keeps glancing back toward Bornanew as he explains the story of one or another object: A captain's uniform of the Fourteenth Battlegroup, sword and all, from the period of the Cold Passage; a bottle of spirits from some core Domain star system near Old Earth; a pistol once owned by the Warlord Leonis; the crystalline xenocoral of a species extinct for half a billion years collected by the Exploraria.
options:
bffi_mpSteal9a:"Astounding. Makes one think how we would handle the Cold Passage. Menes?"
bffi_mpSteal9b:"Incredible. What would it take to persuade you to hold a tasting? Menes?"
bffi_mpSteal9c:"Fascinating. Do you think Leonis shot anyone with this, Menes?"
bffi_mpSteal9d:"Intriguing. I wonder if I might find more such xenolife on the fringe. Menes?"

text: "Huh? Oh yes, naturally I've ruminated often upon how my own character might be tested by such hardship. I admit, my life has been one of relative ease, but I like to think that I identify with certain qualities of those early heroic figures whose steadfastness effected the..."

"Ah? Oh yes, I cannot deny the temptation to open it for some appropriate event, given an appropriately appreciative audience- my dear cousin Horus for instance; I considered his marriage to Asherah, but I thought- no, not yet. I wonder at times if the delight of it is held by the anticipation, the thought..."

"Oh? Oh, well, I do have a story for you! During negotiations with the buyer I thought, let us have a firearms expert examine this, and wouldn't you know that I happened to be hosting a wealthy financier who's done some work with Tri-Tachyon - and say what you will for the Company, they are good at what they do - and she happened to have a brother with an interest in a firm..."

"Eh? Ah! Well I daresay, it is you who might answer that question fully, though I've always considered myself something of a naturalist, or xeno-naturalist if you will ha-ha, and so what if I were to finance an expedition of discovery of sorts? 'Menes Yaribay, patron of knowledge'; I like the sound of that. Imagine, to explore new worlds, encounter unknown lifeforms, seek out and explore the far fringes..."

He rambles, and you make a good show of asking for additional details, drawing his attention toward the object at hand and away from whatever in Ludd's name Bornanew is doing.

A piercing alarm interrupts Menes mid-thought. His eyes go wide and snap toward the central atrium which holds the glove- and Bornanew. He takes off at a run.

You both find Bornanew, still on his knees, with one hand stretched out to touch the glove - one finger upon it - lips still moving, eyes still blankly huge as if overcome by religious mania.

Menes gives out a wordless cry of alarm, and begins lifting his cuff to call security.

Your off-hand instinctively curls around a control interface for one of the clever little thieves-tools you have hidden about your uniform.
options:
bffi_mpSteal12a:"$global.jethroName! Restrain yourself, man!"
bffi_mpSteal12b:"He's only touched it, no need for-"

text:
Menes hesitates as you speak, then barks a codeword into his comms.

You freeze, understanding what this sort of protocol looks like. The lights flare into harsh illumination and a trio of gun turrets pop from subtle cupolas - one each tracks instantly to you and Bornanew while the third spins in a slow covering pattern.

The human guards arrive a moment later, uniforms bright-white, boots squeaking on the floor. Menes breathes heavily, watching the security net tighten.

Bornanew, meanwhile, has not moved but to turn his head toward you and Menes.
"Menes, Yaribay sir. I pray your forgiveness. I- I was overcome by... I had to..." Bornanew trails off, pathetically.
options:
bffi_mpSteal14a:Ne rien dire
bffi_mpSteal14b:(whisper) "This'll make a great story."

text:
It takes a second, but Menes starts to laugh.

And laugh, and laugh deep in his belly, almost a shout. He suddenly throws an arm over your shoulder, putting you off balance, as he struggles to contain himself.
"You're right!" he shouts, "Captain, by the stars, you are right."

"You there," Menes calls out, "Knight $global.jethroName, get yourself off the floor. Come wash your face, you look absolutely disgusting. Back to the party! Drinks are on me, ha-ha!"

As almost an afterthought he calls to the security team, "You lot, clean this up and get out of here... ruining the mood."

text:
You and Bornanew, he given a basin and washcloth by a servitor, are hauled back into the party, and the entire crowd regaled with the story of Bornanew being overcome with awe at the relic and then caught setting off the security alarm.

Menes seems to find it more hilarious with every telling - and each additional round of drinks. His dutiful entourage joins in the laughter each time. The skinny man, Ulmus Pond, has a particularly manic, piercing laugh.

It is some time before you are able to get a quiet word in with $global.jethroName.

bffi_mpSteal17b:"What went wrong?"

text:
"I beg pardon for your blasphemy, captain," Bornanew says with a hint of strain in his voice. "Ludd's conception of hell was, so much as the Word reveals, a description of mortal torment in a Domain without God."

"But I do have the glove. What remains is-" an almost malicious smile briefly appears, "a fake."

"Little, but for the monotony of my humiliation, however justly earned," Bornanew says with a hint of strain in his voice. "But I do have the glove. What remains is a-" an almost malicious smile very briefly appears, "fake."
He lifts a finger to his lips, and with his other hand pats a fold in his Knight's robes.

bffi_mpSteal18c:"... Praise Ludd."

text:
His eyes keep watch on Menes as he replies.

"It's just a glove, captain. The color matches. I didn't think Menes would notice." His mouth quirks. "He didn't."

"He will notice eventually, however. We should... become distant from this place."

* * *

It is a feat of diplomacy that you manage to extract yourself and Bornanew as quickly as you are able.

A faked containment failure alert from your chief engineer merely opens the door. Passing through requires shaking everyone's hand, exchanging pleasant words, and going through the motions of good-byes several times over - then each time the ritual is interrupted by some tangent or anecdote from Menes, he ensures that it is begun again from the start.

At last you find yourself back in your shuttle, a half-full glass still in your hand.

text:
As you commit to the lie, Bornanew gives you a look of absolute weariness.

Menes turns to him, "This is true? This is why you're here, isn't it. The backing of the Church, of course!"

"Yes," Bornanew says robotically, his attempt at a pleasant countenance looks like an appalling mask of pain.

Even Menes seems to sense something is off. "Are you feeling well, Knight?" he asks. "Perhaps you aren't used to the quality of drink I serve."

Bornanew closes his eyes, and you hear his sigh of relief.

Menes glances at him, giving a funny look, then back at you. "Ah, of course. Ha-ha." He clears his throat. "Odd folk, you spacers," he says as if to himself.
Menes smirks, and holds out his TriPad, ready to accept your transfer code. Perhaps he feels like he's calling your bluff.
